report_name: Purple Knight Security Tool Adds Support for Microsoft GCC High Environments

tools.name: Purple Knight
tools.description: A free identity security assessment tool from Semperis that scans on-premises Active Directory and cloud-based Entra ID tenants for misconfigurations and vulnerabilities. It is recommended by the Five Eyes intelligence alliance.

tools.name: Semperis Directory Services Protector
tools.description: A commercial platform from Semperis that provides continuous monitoring and automated response for identity system security issues.

identities.name: U.S. Federal Agencies
identities.description: Users of Microsoft's Government Community Cloud High (GCC High) environments who can now use the Purple Knight tool to assess their Entra ID tenants.

identities.name: U.S. Department of Defense
identities.description: Organizations operating within the GCC High environment that can now use Purple Knight for Entra ID security assessments.

identities.name: Defense Industrial Base
identities.description: Contractors who operate within the GCC High environment and can now use Purple Knight to scan their Entra ID tenants.

identities.name: Five Eyes Intelligence Alliance
identities.description: An intelligence alliance that recommends the use of Purple Knight for hardening Active Directory environments.

identities.name: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency
identities.description: A U.S. government agency, part of the Five Eyes intelligence alliance, that recommends the Purple Knight tool.

identities.name: National Security Agency
identities.description: A U.S. government agency, part of the Five Eyes intelligence alliance, that recommends the Purple Knight tool.

indicators.name: Kerberoasting Service Ticket Request
indicators.pattern:
title: Kerberoasting RC4 Service Ticket Request
id: 2b1a8c3d-9e4f-4b1a-8c3d-9e4f4b1a8c3d
status: experimental
description: Detects a Kerberos service ticket request (TGS-REQ) using the weak RC4-HMAC encryption type, which is indicative of a Kerberoasting attack. This detection is based on the recommendation to create targeted SIEM rules for vulnerabilities found by Purple Knight.
references:
  - https://www.semperis.com/resources/webinars/emergency-communications-org-closes-ad-security-gaps-with-purple-knight/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-21
modified: 2026-04-21
tags:
  - attack.t1558.003
  - attack.credential_access
level: high
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: security
detection:
  selection:
    EventID: 4769
    TicketEncryptionType: '0x17'
  filter_machine_accounts:
    AccountName|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection and not filter_machine_accounts
indicators.description: The article suggests that if Purple Knight identifies accounts vulnerable to Kerberoasting, a detection rule can be created to alert on the specific event IDs associated with that attack.
